reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N–O bond rearrangement in nitrile oxide generation; loss of H2O from ethyl nitroacetate

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents are premixed in DES; phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide forms in situ to trap it and prevent dimerization. No incompatibility between substrates at room temperature before heating. Single feed simplifies pumping of viscous DES.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ upon heating; 2 equiv used to drive reaction to completion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent; acts as reaction medium and may facilitate nitrile oxide generation

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal dehydration/tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) in situ, facilitated by the DES medium at 120°C

Regioselective [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide 1,3-dipole with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate. The oxygen of the nitrile oxide attacks the terminal carbon of the alkyne, and the carbon of the nitrile oxide bonds to the internal carbon, giving the 3,5-disubstituted isoxazole regioselectively.

key_intermediate: ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (in-situ generated nitrile oxide from ethyl nitroacetate)

stream_logic.reasoning: All components premixed in a single stream. Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate are stable together at room temperature; the nitrile oxide only forms upon heating in the reactor. Premixing ensures the dipolarophile is present to trap the transient nitrile oxide immediately, suppressing dimerization to furoxan. DES is viscous (~50 cP at RT) so a single syringe pump with heated lines or pre-warming to ~50°C aids pumping.

mixing_order_reasoning: Single premixed feed is optimal. Ethyl nitroacetate and phenylacetylene are mutually stable at ambient temperature. The nitrile oxide intermediate only forms at elevated temperature (≥100°C), so premixing before the heated reactor zone ensures immediate trapping of the 1,3-dipole by the alkyne, minimizing nitrile oxide dimerization (furoxan byproduct).
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species; the reaction proceeds thermally without radical intermediates that would be quenched by O2.
quench_reasoning: No reactive intermediates persist after the short residence time at 120°C; the product is stable. Offline aqueous workup and extraction with DCM suffice for purification.
similar_reaction_classes: isoxazoline synthesis from nitrile oxides and alkenes
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photoinitiation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism: well-established nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate and [3+2] cycloaddition. The DES viscosity (~50 cP at RT, lower at 120°C) is a practical concern for flow pumping — may need heated feed lines or dilution. Backpressure regulator recommended to keep DES/EG below boiling at 120°C. The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate suggests incomplete nitrile oxide generation efficiency; in flow, tighter temperature control may allow reduction toward 1.5 equiv. Residence time of 15 min at 120°C is straightforward for a heated coil reactor (e.g., 1.0 mm ID PFA tubing, ~0.8 mL volume at 50 µL/min). Uncertain whether DES recycling is feasible in continuous mode.
